items.title: CMS slashes dispute fees, adds new rules under No Surprises Act - MSN
items.reason: CMS rulemaking that modifies dispute fees and procedures under the No Surprises Act directly impacts the IDR process and revenue-cycle operations.
items.summary: CMS has reduced dispute resolution fees and implemented new procedural rules under the No Surprises Act's Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) process. The fee reduction and updated requirements affect how healthcare providers and payers participate in federal dispute resolution for out-of-network claims. This rulemaking directly impacts the cost and operational burden of the IDR process for revenue-cycle teams managing balance-billing disputes and claim appeals under the No Surprises Act framework.

items.title: Can Elevance Recover $115 Million Through Its CMS Lawsuit? - Yahoo Finance
items.reason: This item reports on litigation involving Elevance (a major payer) and CMS with a specific dollar amount at stake, which directly matches the topic's focus on payer litigation and regulatory enforcement actions.
items.summary: Elevance Health is pursuing a lawsuit against CMS seeking recovery of approximately $115 million. The lawsuit represents a regulatory dispute between a major national health insurer and the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, likely involving Medicare Advantage or related program issues. Revenue-cycle teams should monitor this case as CMS enforcement actions and payer disputes with regulators can affect reimbursement policies, payment terms, and operational requirements across the healthcare system.
